<!-- To regenerate: alef docs -->
<!-- To verify freshness: alef verify --exit-code -->

# Crawlberg > High-performance Rust web crawling engine with generated language packages. Crawl, scrape, map, interact
with browser-backed pages, stream crawl events, and convert HTML to Markdown. Usable as a Rust crate, generated package,
CLI, REST API, MCP server, or Docker image. Crawlberg is built around a trait-based crawl engine and a Tower middleware
stack. It ships BFS / DFS / BestFirst / Adaptive traversal strategies, aggregate batch wrappers, streaming events,
sitemap and link-graph discovery, JSON-LD parsing, SSRF policy checks, and WAF signal classification with hot-reloadable
rules. ## Get Started - [Installation](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/getting-started/installation/): Install Crawlberg
for Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, Kotlin, C#, Ruby, PHP, Elixir, Dart, Swift, Zig, WebAssembly, or via CLI /
Docker - [Quick Start](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/getting-started/quickstart/): Scrape a single page, crawl a site,
and map URLs in under five minutes - [AI Coding
Assistants](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/ai-coding-assistants/): Install the plugin from the xberg-io/plugins
marketplace — ships agent skills plus an MCP server, with per-harness install steps for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor,
Gemini, Factory Droid, Copilot, and opencode ## Guides - [Crawling](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/crawling/):
Multi-page traversal with depth and page limits, batch crawl -
[Scraping](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/scraping/): Single-page extraction and batch scrape - [URL
Discovery](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/url-discovery/): Sitemap parsing, link extraction, and map_urls -
[Markdown Conversion](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/markdown/): Always-on HTML→Markdown via
[html-to-markdown](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io), citations, and pruning - [Browser
Automation](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/browser/): Chromiumoxide and native browser backends, WAF escalation,
persistent profiles - [WARC Output](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/warc/): Standards-compliant WARC archiving -
[Antibot Strategy](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/antibot/): WAF detection, escalation tiers, pluggable classifier
traits - [Configuration](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/configuration/): CrawlConfig, ContentConfig,
BrowserConfig, AuthConfig, ProxyConfig - [Docker](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/docker/): Container image with
feature flags - [API Server](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/api-server/): Firecrawl v1-compatible REST API -
[MCP Server](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/guides/mcp-server/): MCP tools for AI agent integration -
[Observability](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/observability/): OpenTelemetry traces and metrics, W3C trace
propagation, span catalogue ## CLI - [CLI Usage](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/cli/usage/): Command-line reference for
the `crawlberg` binary ## Concepts - [Architecture](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/concepts/architecture/): Public
surface, data flow, binding matrix, feature gates, crate layout - [Content
Extraction](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/concepts/content-extraction/): HTML parsing, metadata, Markdown conversion,
citations, content pruning - [SSRF Defense](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/concepts/ssrf-defense/): Host allowlists,
private-IP rejection, redirect validation, opt-out policy ## API Reference - [Rust
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-rust/): Core crate, `create_engine`, `crawl`, `scrape`, `map_urls`,
`interact`, citations, batch operations, streaming APIs - [Python
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-python/): PyO3 bindings with asyncio support - [TypeScript
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-typescript/): NAPI-RS bindings for Node.js - [Go
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-go/): cgo bindings over the C FFI surface - [Java
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-java/): Panama FFM bindings - [Kotlin (Android)
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-kotlin-android/): Android AAR with JNA + bundled JNI .so files - [C#
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-csharp/): P/Invoke bindings for .NET - [Ruby
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-ruby/): Magnus bindings - [PHP
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-php/): ext-php-rs bindings - [Elixir
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-elixir/): Rustler NIF - [Dart
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-dart/): Dart FFI bindings - [Swift
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-swift/): Swift package over the C FFI surface - [Zig
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-zig/): Zig wrapper over the C FFI surface - [WebAssembly
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-wasm/): wasm-bindgen surface for browsers and edge runtimes - [C /
FFI API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/api-c/): C header + shared library - [REST
API](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/rest-api/): HTTP endpoints for the API server - [MCP
Tools](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/mcp-tools/): Tool catalogue for the MCP server -
[Configuration](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/configuration/): Full configuration schema and defaults -
[Types](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/types/): `CrawlResult`, `ScrapeResult`, `MapResult`, `PageMetadata`,
and related types - [Errors](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/reference/errors/): `CrawlError` variants and FFI error
codes ## Xberg Ecosystem - [Xberg](https://docs.xberg.io): Document intelligence: text, tables, metadata from 91+
formats with optional OCR - [Xberg Enterprise](https://enterprise.xberg.io): Managed extraction API with SDKs,
dashboards, and observability - [html-to-markdown](https://docs.html-to-markdown.xberg.io): Fast, lossless HTML to
Markdown engine - [liter-llm](https://docs.liter-llm.xberg.io): Universal LLM API client with native bindings for 14
languages and 143 providers - [tree-sitter-language-pack](https://docs.tree-sitter-language-pack.xberg.io): Tree-sitter
grammars and code-intelligence primitives ## Optional - [Features](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/features/): Complete
feature breakdown - [Contributing](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/contributing/): How to contribute -
[Changelog](https://docs.crawlberg.xberg.io/CHANGELOG/): Release history

## Generated CLI Inventory

  - `crawlberg`: High-performance web crawler and scraper
    - `crawlberg batch-crawl`: Crawl multiple websites concurrently
    - `crawlberg batch-scrape`: Scrape multiple URLs concurrently
    - `crawlberg citations`: Convert markdown links into numbered citations
    - `crawlberg crawl`: Crawl a website following links
    - `crawlberg download`: Download a document from a URL and report its metadata
    - `crawlberg interact`: Execute browser actions on a single page
    - `crawlberg map`: Discover all URLs on a website via sitemaps and link extraction
    - `crawlberg mcp`: Start the MCP server (stdio transport)
    - `crawlberg scrape`: Scrape a single URL and extract metadata
    - `crawlberg serve`: Start the REST API server
    - `crawlberg version`: Print the crawlberg version as JSON

## Generated MCP Inventory

  - `batch_crawl`: Crawl multiple seed URLs concurrently. Returns crawl results for all seeds.
  - `batch_scrape`: Scrape multiple URLs concurrently. Returns results for all URLs.
  - `crawl`: Crawl a website following links. Returns content for all discovered pages up to max_depth/max_pages.
  - `download`: Download a document from a URL. Returns metadata about the downloaded file.
  - `generate_citations`: Convert markdown links into numbered citations with an appended reference list.
  - `get_version`: Get the current crawlberg library version.
  - `interact`: Execute browser actions on a page. Actions may mutate page or application state.
  - `map`: Discover all pages on a website via links and sitemaps. Returns a list of discovered URLs.
  - `scrape`: Scrape a single URL and extract content as markdown or JSON. Returns page content, metadata, links, and images.
